Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Hardware hotplug events on Linux, the gory details 128 upvotes, 9 comments
  2. Let's Get Physical 113 upvotes, 17 comments
  3. A ternary plot of citrus geneology 108 upvotes, 17 comments
  4. OpenTitan Shipping in Production 90 upvotes, 14 comments
  5. GLiNER2: Unified Schema-Based Information Extraction 45 upvotes, 5 comments
  6. Show HN: Jido 2.0, Elixir Agent Framework 259 upvotes, 56 comments
  7. A GitHub Issue Title Compromised 4k Developer Machines 358 upvotes, 86 comments
  8. Converting dash cam videos into Panoramax images 42 upvotes, 7 comments
  9. Hacking Super Mario 64 using covering spaces 21 upvotes, 2 comments
  10. Wikipedia was in read-only mode following mass admin account compromise 919 upvotes, 319 comments
  11. A standard protocol to handle and discard low-effort, AI-Generated pull requests 122 upvotes, 37 comments
  12. CBP tapped into the online advertising ecosystem to track peoples’ movements 410 upvotes, 165 comments
  13. The next generations of Bubble Tea, Lip Gloss, and Bubbles are available now 158 upvotes, 60 comments
  14. Proton Mail Helped FBI Unmask Anonymous 'Stop Cop City' Protester 279 upvotes, 138 comments
  15. Good software knows when to stop 376 upvotes, 200 comments
  16. 10% of Firefox crashes are caused by bitflips 374 upvotes, 199 comments
  17. Remotely unlocking an encrypted hard disk 105 upvotes, 54 comments
  18. Show HN: PageAgent, A GUI agent that lives inside your web app 80 upvotes, 39 comments
  19. How to install and start using LineageOS on your phone 26 upvotes, 10 comments
  20. Judge orders government to begin refunding more than $130B in tariffs 864 upvotes, 638 comments
  21. Stop Using Grey Text (2025) 43 upvotes, 25 comments
  22. GPT-5.4 709 upvotes, 613 comments
  23. Nobody ever got fired for using a struct 54 upvotes, 34 comments
  24. The Brand Age 275 upvotes, 236 comments
  25. Where things stand with the Department of War 305 upvotes, 275 comments
  26. Ethiopia gets $350M World Bank financing for its digital ID project (2024) 45 upvotes, 34 comments
  27. Show HN: Swarm – Program a colony of 200 ants using a custom assembly language 8 upvotes, 3 comments
  28. Labor market impacts of AI: A new measure and early evidence 108 upvotes, 124 comments
  29. AI and the Ship of Theseus 68 upvotes, 77 comments
  30. Structured AI (YC F25) Is Hiring 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 1:00pm, 6 March 2026. Source here.
